sections.paragraph:
(cogn. to לָהָה)-

(1) pr. to labour (see Niph.).

(2) to be wearied, to be exhausted. Job 4:5, “because calamity now toucheth thee, וַתֵּלָא thou faintest;” followed by לּ with inf. to labour in vain, not to be able, Gen 19:11.

(3) to be weary of anything, to be offended at, Job 4:2.

Niphal, i.q. Kal, but of more frequent use-

(1) to labour, followed by an inf. Jer 9:4, הַעֲוֵה נִלְאוּ “they labour to act perversely,” especially “to labour in vain;” Jer 20:9 Isa 16:12.

(2) to be wearied, to be exhausted, Psa 68:10 followed by בְּ Isa 47:13.

(3) to be weary of anything; followed by an inf., Isa 1:14 Jer 6:11 15:6 followed by a gerund, Pro 26:15, “it grieveth him (the sluggard) to bring back his hand to his mouth.” Used of loathing, Exo 7:18.

Hiphil הֶלְאָה-

(1) to weary, to fatigue, Job 16:7 Eze 24:12.

(2) to weary out, or overcome any one’s patience, Isa 7:13 Mic 6:3.

Derivatives, תְּלָאָה and-
